properties.remark: TREE DOWN ACROSS ROAD NEAR CHESTER.
SEVERAL LARGE TREES DOWN IN THE RAYBURN BROWNDELL AREA.
A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE MYSTIC BAPTIST CHURCH. PART OF THE METAL ROOF WAS PEELED OFF AND SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.3 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 20 YARDS.
SHERIFFS DEPT REPORTS TREES DOWN ON HWY 27 NORTHWEST OF ORETTA.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF RAGLEY...DAMAGING 10 TO 25 HOMES. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS TO ROOFING MATERIAL...ALTHOUGH SOME HOMES HAD TREES LAND ON THEM. SEVERAL GARAGES AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. ALONG US HIGHWAY 171...SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN WITH DEBRIS BLOCKING THE SOUTHBOUND LANES. PATH LENGTH WAS 2.8 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.
LOCAL TELEVISION STATION DISPLAYED SEVERAL POWER POLES DOWN AND SHED DESTROYED ON HWY 171.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND BUILDING DAMAGE ACROSS TOWN.
PICTURE OF BARN WITH ROOF BLOWN OFF ON SOCAIAL MEDIA. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

E 911 SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED IN LECOMPTE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ON L D VERRETTE LANE...DAMAGING THREE HOMES.ALL THREE HAD ROOF DAMAGE...WITH PARTIAL METAL ROOFING MATERIAL REMOVED. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 25 YARDS.
A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG LLOYDS BRIDGE ROAD...DAMAGING SEVERAL HOMES AND LARGE BARNS. DEBRIS WAS STREWN ACROSS ADJACENT FIELDS. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.4 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.

A TORNADO FORMED OVER BUNKIE AND PRODUCED WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE...ESPECIALLY ON THE WESTERN SIDE OF TOWN. TWO HOMES WERE DESTROYED WHEN TREES FELL ON THEM. AT LEAST 70 BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.6 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS.
ROOF DAMAGE TO AND LARGE BRANCHES DOWN.
EM REPORTS NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERPOLES DOWN. ONE HOUSE PHYSICALLY MOVED.

WIDESPREAD TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ACROSS THE HESSMER COMMUNITY. AT LEAST 30 BUILDINGS WERE DMAGED OR DESTROYED. NUMEROUS POWER LINES AND POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG MAJOR ROADS INCLUDING LA HWY 114 AND MAIN STREET. A MOVING TRUCK WAS TIPPED ON ITS SIDE BY THE WIND. PATH LENGTH WAS 2.4 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS.
SEMI TRUCK BLOWN OVER. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SOCIAL MEDIA PICTURES SHOW TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AT HWY 361 AND 29. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
TREE DOWN ACROSS CHARLENE HWY.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE AIRPORT WHERE ONE HANGAR AND TWO PLANES WERE DAMAGED. A WAL-MART STORE HAD SOME SKYLIGHTS SHATTERED AND PARTS OF THE ROOF WAS DAMAGED. NEIGHBORING STRIP MALL STORES HAD MINOR FACADE DAMAGE AND A PORTABLE FIREWORKS STAND WAS DESTROYED. ACROSS THE STREET...SEVERAL LARGE TREES FELL ON HOMES AND SEVERAL OTHER BUIDLINGS WERE DAMAGED AS WELL. PATH LENGTH WAS 1 MILE. PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS.
SEMI TRUCK TRAILER BLOWN ON SIDE. CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE AT WALMART PER SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND METAL ROOFS WERE DAMAGED ON SEVERAL BUILDINGS.

1 TREE FELL AND PARTIALLY DAMAGED 2 HOUSES AND A CARPORT/CAR OFF GENERAL DRIVE IN NORTH BEAUMONT. SURFACE OBSERVATIONS ACROSS THE AREA REPORTED WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 40 AND 45 MPH AS GUST FRONT MOVED THROUGH. OTHER ISOLATED DOWNED TREES REPORTED THROUGH THE COUNTY.

TREE DOWN OFF GOSS FERRY ROAD. TIME BASED ON RADAR. SURFACE OBSERVATIONS REPORTED WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 40 AND 50 MPH AS THE GUST FRONT MOVED ACROSS THE AREA. KLCH RADAR SHOWED BASE VELOCITY UPTICK TO 42 KNOTS OR 48 MPH AT 700 FEET AT THAT LOCATION AS CELL MOVED ACROSS...BUT NO DISCERNABLE ROTATION.

FACEBOOK POST OF A TREE DOWN OFF ADAMS ROAD EAST OF TOPSY. OTHER TREES NEARBY HAD TOPS OF THEM BLOWN DOWN. TIME BASED ON RADAR. SURFACE OBSERVATIONS REPORTED WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 40 AND 50 MPH AS THE GUST FRONT MOVED THROUGH. KLCH RADAR SHOWED BASE VELOCITY UPTICK OF 41 KNOTS OR 47 MPH AT 1300 FEET AT THAT LOCATION AS CELL MOVED ACROSS...BUT NO DISCERNABLE ROTATION.
REPORT FROM PLATFORM IN EAST CAMERON BLOCK 14.

HAM RADIO REPORT OF SEVERAL TREES DOWN OFF POTTER ROAD IN ALLEN PARISH. SURFACE OBSERVATIONS REPORTED WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 40 AND 50 MPH AS GUST FRONT MOVED ACROSS THE AREA. KLCH RADAR SHOWED BASE VELOCITY UPTICK TO 38 KNOTS OR 44 MPH AT 2000 FEET AT THAT TIME AND LOCATION...BUT NO DISCERNABLE ROTATION.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF OAKDALE NEAR THE WAL-MART DAMAGING THE GAS STATION CANOPY. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED NORTHEAST CROSSING 165 AND MOVED THROUGH OAKDALE BLOWING DOWN OR SNAPPING NUMEROUS LARGE TREE. SOME TREES LANDED ON HOMES AND BUSINESSES. SOME BUILDINGS RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGE FROM THE TORNADO, ESPECIALLY NEAR THE ICE FACILITY WHERE THE TORNADO WAS MOST INTESNE.

KPLC RELAED A REPORT FROM THE OAKDALE CHIEF OF POLICE OF DAMAGE RELATED TO A POSSIBLE TORNADO THAT INCLUDED TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 165, A ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A MURPHY GAS STATION AND TREES DOWN ON MAPLE ST.

TORNADO STARTED SOUTH OF LA 115 ALONG HAASVILLE RD. RIPPED TIN OFF OUT BUILDINGS AND SNAPPED TREE LIMBS. AS THE TORNADO CROSSED LA 115 IT STRENGTHENED TOPPLING A LARGE TREE ONTO A CARPORT DAMAGING IT AND SEVERAL VEHICLES. OTHER TREES WERE ALSO TOPPLED IN THE DAMAGE PATH. A DAYCARE BUSINESS NEXT DOOR ALSO HAD METAL ROOF RIPPED OFF.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** BEAUMONT PD REPORTS A CAR WAS TRAVELING SOUTH ON A SERVICE ROAD WHEN IT HIT HIGH WATER AND BECAME STUCK. THE WOMAN EXITED THE VEHCILE WITH HER CHILD AND SOME TIME AFTERWARDS THE TWO WERE SWEPT INTO A CANAL. THE CHILD WAS RESCUED BUT WAS SUFFERING FROM HYPOTHERMIA. THE MOTHER WAS UNRESPONSIVE AND DIED.
